phase_status_legend.armed: phase eligible to run this tick
phase_status_legend.paused_by_escalation: see escalations[] for the L5 tag pausing this phase; auto-retries when next_retry_at <= now

queue.title: `harness replay` CLI verb - decision archaeology for dispatches
queue.description: CLI verb reading state/jsonl_log + cost ledger for a task_id; renders dispatch timeline (engines tried, guards fired, fallbacks, redaction events). Satisfactory-flavored UX. Foundation for Wave 3 dashboard.
queue.note: Needs operator approval before dispatch; wave_plan items take priority

parked_ideas.title: Dispatch budget meter + per-engine cost ledger
Gemini 2.x engine adapter for triangulation tier

wave_plan.name: retrofit raises across codebase to HarnessError subclasses
wave_plan.completion_note: Scope refined during execution: 4 raises retrofitted (1 in dpapi.py to ConfigCorruption, 3 in jsonl_log.py to SchemaViolation). Pydantic validators, user-input validation in loader, dispatcher's contract-protected raise (L81), and assertion-style RuntimeErrors intentionally left as ValueError/RuntimeError — they don't fit the L3-L5 operational error model. 89/89 tests still green.

wave_plan.name: boundary tests for engines/concrete.py + engines/guards.py + GitHub Actions CI
wave_plan.next_strategy: Wait for Kimi CLI cooldown (~25 min remaining); dispatch the 2 test files as ONE Kimi CLI packet (interdependent fixtures); CI already gates regressions.

autonomous loops as harness feature (productize dev_loop pattern)
wave_plan.note: Generalize coord/dev_loop/ scaffolding into adapter-YAML-configurable feature with per-supervisor engine choice and Task Scheduler integration
wave_plan.scope_expansion_2026_05_20: Must include operator-modes config surface: every directive given in this dev-loop session becomes a discoverable CLI flag / YAML key. See memory feedback_operator_inputs_become_harness_config for the directive table (full-dev-authority, L5 escalation, parallelism, engine-fill, etc.).

wave_plan.name: operator-mode CLI flags + adapter.operator YAML section
wave_plan.note: Surface session directives as harness config (--full-dev-authority, --escalation-threshold L5, --engine-fill aggressive, etc.). Foundation for Wave 6. Smaller scope than full productization.

engine_slots._doc: Each tick, the manager fills idle Kimi slots with queued work. DeepSeek stays idle unless explicitly needed (cost-per-API constraint).

kimi-api.note: second Kimi transport; use when kimi (CLI) slots full

deepseek.note: DeepSeek costs per API call; only dispatch when task needs its strengths (V-file, math/schema, novel-feature drafting)

dispatch_learnings.insight: xaxiu-swarm backend agentic capability differs: 'kimi' (CLI) is agentic and can apply in-place edits via Edit/Write tools. 'kimi-api' and 'deepseek' are NOT agentic — they produce a single text response written to --deliverable path. Packets requesting in-place edits must target kimi CLI; packets to api backends must request FIND/REPLACE or full-file-contents output the integrating supervisor parses.
evidence: Wave A.6 on deepseek-v4-flash: exit 0, prose output, no files modified
Wave B on kimi-api: exit 0, test code in output but not written to disk
